attributes.action: Protect Care Worker's Settlement Rights - Keep or Reduce the 5-Year Route to ILR
attributes.additional_details: Care workers are essential to the UK’s adult social care system but are among the lowest-paid workers in healthcare. The sector already faces severe staff shortages and relies heavily on international workers. Extending the route to settlement beyond 5 years would create more financial and emotional pressure and may worsen recruitment and retention in the care sector.
attributes.background: Ensure care workers remain eligible for Indefinite Leave to Remain after 5 years and are not included in any proposals to extend settlement to 10 years. Consider introducing a shorter settlement pathway in recognition of their vital role in UK social care.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petitions, which call for similar action:

Allow care workers to access 5-year ILR route like other health and care workers
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/754848

Maintain the current 5-year route to (ILR) for Senior Carers & Care Assistants
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/752023

Keep 5-year ILR for Health and Care visa holders to protect the NHS
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/745684
